# mistlib-wasm (vendored)

This directory is generated by `scripts/build-mistlib.sh` (`npm run build:mistlib`).
It is intentionally empty in a fresh checkout — the mistlib source repo is
private, and its URL lives only in your local `.env` (never committed).

To vendor the wasm module:

1. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and set `MISTLIB_REPO` (and optionally
   `MISTLIB_REF`) to the mistlib source repository.
2. Run `npm run build:mistlib`.

This produces `mistlib_wasm.js`, `mistlib_wasm.d.ts`, `mistlib_wasm_bg.wasm`,
`mistlib_wasm_bg.wasm.d.ts`, and `BUILD_INFO.txt` in this directory.

Until vendored, the app still runs: P2P features are guarded behind a
dynamic `import()` with a try/catch, so the rest of the viewer (local VRM
loading, bundle import, the model library) works without it. The P2P panel
shows a setup hint instead of a room join form when the module is missing.
